pfs.note: All characters have access to raise dead

spell.cost: diamonds worth a total value of the target's level (minimum 1) x 200 gp

heightened.text: The maximum level of the target increases to 15. The cost increases to the target's level (minimum 1) × 400 gp.
The maximum level of the target increases to 17. The cost increases to the target's level (minimum 1) × 800 gp.
The maximum level of the target increases to 19. The cost increases to the target's level (minimum 1) × 1,600 gp.
The maximum level of the target increases to 21. The cost increases to the target's level (minimum 1) × 3,200 gp.

spell.name: Raise Dead
spell.targets: 1 dead creature of 13th level or lower
spell.text:
You attempt to call forth the dead creature's soul, requiring the creature's body to be present and relatively intact. The creature must have died within the past 3 days. If Pharasma has decided that the creature's time has come (at the GM's discretion), or if the creature doesn't wish to return to life, this spell automatically fails, but the diamonds aren't consumed in the casting.  
  
 If the spell is successful, the creature returns to life with 1 Hit Point, no spells prepared or spell slots available, no points in any pools or any other daily resources, and still with any long-term debilitations of the old body. The time spent in the Boneyard leaves the target temporarily debilitated, making it clumsy 2, drained 2, and enfeebled 2 for 1 week; these conditions can't be removed or reduced by any means until the week has passed. The creature is also permanently changed by its time in the afterlife, such as a slight personality shift, a streak of white in the hair, or a strange new birthmark.

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Healing
traits.text: A healing effect restores a creature's body, typically by restoring Hit Points, but sometimes by removing diseases or other debilitating effects.

traits.name: Necromancy
traits.text: Effects and magic items with this trait are associated with the necromancy school of magic, typically involving forces of life and death.
